response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled individual contributor and Staff Software Engineer with strong distributed-systems and backend credentials, but the profile does not meet the core requirements for this CTO role. There is no evidence of executive leadership, no track record of scaling and managing engineering teams of 10–30+ people, no experience governing outsourced engineering partnerships, and no demonstrated ownership of product/technical strategy at an organizational level. His AI-agent exposure at re:cinq is recent and framed as contributory rather than architectural or leadership-level. The fintech experience at RIDE Capital is limited to IC-level software craftsmanship. The seniority gap between the candidate's current trajectory and the expectations of this role is too wide to bridge through an interview process.
strengths: Genuine distributed-systems and high-throughput backend experience at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/second, low-latency microservices)
Early, hands-on exposure to agentic-AI infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq, signaling awareness of the relevant technology space
Broad polyglot background across TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and cloud tooling (AWS CDK, Docker, Kubernetes) indicating strong technical range
concerns: Zero executive or senior engineering-management experience — no record of owning a technical roadmap, managing technical leads, or reporting to a board
No demonstrated track record of scaling or building an engineering organization; all roles are individual-contributor or small-team founder contexts
AI/agentic-systems involvement is very recent (<2 months at re:cinq) and described at a contributory level, far short of the production multi-agent architecture ownership this role requires

key_factors.factor: AI/agentic experience too shallow and recent
Solid distributed-systems and high-traffic backend background
